grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:225 defines the legacy B_d mixing input key b_d.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:231 defines the legacy b_d bound used by the v1 input bundle.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:236 notes B_d mixing with proper hadronic matrix elements.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:631-638 stores the in-code B_d hadronic constants and experimental Delta m_Bd conversion.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:383-391 dispatches b_d evaluation and per-operator size bookkeeping.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:888-890 defines the B_d NP budget.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903-915 evaluates the B_d mixing NP contribution.
quarkConstraints/qcd_running.py:7 documents the Delta F=2 operator basis.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 includes B_d in the modern policy system IDs.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:646-654 defines the modern B_d bridge evaluator.
quarkConstraints/modern/inputs.py:967-976 mirrors the B_d default input in the modern metadata layer.
tests/test_quark_deltaf2.py:120-131 checks stable Delta F=2 outputs including b_d.

cfw_2008_rs_flavor.use: RS-flavor baseline for post-2008 comparison; not a measured experimental observable.

CHK-1: TeX still quotes Belle II Delta m_d = 0.516 +/- 0.008(stat) +/- 0.005(syst) ps^-1 and 190 fb^-1 outside pdg_or_equivalent; WA-v2 added complete metadata under recent_experimental_inputs instead.
status_history.reason: Cycle-2 checker found strict CHK-1 pdg_or_equivalent placement gaps; see flavor_catalog/worklogs/checker/ca_w6_beauty_v2.md.

APPROVE-OVERRIDE per L001 / B021_B023 precedent: pdg_or_equivalent is for measured experimental observables. The CFW 21 TeV / 33 TeV RS-flavor reference scales are theoretical normalization values (belong in paper_era_reference), and the Belle II 2023 dataset metadata (190 fb^-1 luminosity and per-experiment Delta m_d) belongs in recent_experimental_inputs. The HFLAV/PDG 2025 canonical Delta m_d = 0.5069 +/- 0.0019 ps^-1 plus x_d, chi_d companions are already in pdg_or_equivalent with full strict metadata. See flavor_catalog/signoff/by_process/B001_B003.md.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\Delta m_d\).
This entry covers the mass splitting of the neutral \(B_d\) eigenstates,
equivalently the oscillation frequency in \(B^0_d-\bar B^0_d\) mixing.  It is
the beauty-sector down-type \(\Delta F=2\) magnitude constraint already used by
the quark-scan code.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical measured value is the HFLAV/PDG 2025 average recorded in
\texttt{B001.yaml} under \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent}, source key
\texttt{hflav\_pdg2025\_bd\_mixing}.  HFLAV gives
\[
  \Delta m_d = 0.5069 \pm 0.0019\ {\rm ps}^{-1},
\]
using time-dependent measurements from ALEPH, DELPHI, L3, OPAL, CDF, D0,
BABAR, Belle, and LHCb.  With the HFLAV assumptions of no CP violation in
mixing and no \(B^0\) width difference, the same snapshot records the companion
world averages \(x_d=0.7697\pm0.0035\) and \(\chi_d=0.1860\pm0.0011\).  The
local source snapshot is
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B001/hflav\_pdg2025\_bd\_mixing.txt},
accessed on 2026-05-16.
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models generate tree-level flavor-changing neutral currents
from KK gluon and electroweak exchange after rotating to the quark-mass basis.
\(B_d\) mixing probes the \(b\leftrightarrow d\) down-sector alignment and is a
direct test of the same Wilson-coefficient pipeline used for kaon and \(B_s\)
mixing.  Compared with \(\varepsilon_K\), it is less chirally enhanced but
constrains a different row-column structure of the anarchic profiles.
sections.post_2008:
The arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline, source key
\texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor}, highlighted KK-gluon flavor bounds near
\(21\,{\rm TeV}\) in standard RS scenarios and \(33\,{\rm TeV}\) in the
pseudo-Goldstone setup, with boundary kinetic terms and flavor assumptions as
caveats.  Since then, \(\Delta m_d\) has become a precision-average observable.
Belle II has repeated the benchmark measurement with early SuperKEKB data,
reporting
\[
  \Delta m_d = 0.516 \pm 0.008_{\rm stat}\pm0.005_{\rm syst}\ {\rm ps}^{-1}
\]
from \(190\,{\rm fb}^{-1}\), with both numbers recorded under source key
\texttt{belleii\_2023\_bd\_mixing}.  On the theory side, source key
\texttt{flag2024\_b\_mixing} documents the FLAG Review 2024 heavy-quark inputs
relevant for \(B\)-mixing matrix elements and global unitarity-triangle fits;
those inputs are theory normalization data, not the measured observable.
sections.validity:
This is a robust neutral-meson-mixing magnitude constraint.  The experimental
average is precise, but turning it into a new-physics exclusion requires a
policy for the SM contribution and CKM inputs.  The current code uses an
allowed NP budget rather than a full correlated CKM fit, so catalog users
should treat it as a conservative magnitude screen, not as a global-fit
replacement.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{YES-D2-BD.}  The legacy \(\Delta F=2\) backend defines the \(B_d\)
input at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:225} and notes the hadronic
matrix-element treatment at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:236}.
The in-code \(B_d\) hadronic and experimental constants are at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:631}--\texttt{638}.  The scan dispatches
\texttt{b\_d} through \texttt{evaluate\_bd\_mixing} at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:383}--\texttt{391}; the evaluator itself is
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903}--\texttt{915}, with the running wrapper
at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:960}--\texttt{966}.  The modern metadata
layer also carries \(B_d\) as a policy system at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} and mirrors the input at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/inputs.py:967}--\texttt{976}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{LOW.}  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR machinery and
BMU-style running cover the \(B_d\) mixing magnitude observable.  A future
production refresh would mainly improve provenance, unit conversion, and
likelihood policy rather than adding new operators or RG evolution.
sections.key_references:
Process-local reference keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{hflav\_pdg2025\_bd\_mixing},
\texttt{belleii\_2023\_bd\_mixing},
\texttt{flag2024\_b\_mixing}, and
\texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor}.
